channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM urges end of NEET after exam paper leak, suggests Class 12 marks for medical admissions
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has called for the immediate abolition of the NEET-UG exam following a major paper leak incident. The National Testing Agency (NTA) had to cancel the 2026 NEE...

item.title: Emily Atack and Cast Shine at Rivals Series Two Premiere in London
item.description: Emily Atack led the arrivals at the Rivals series two premiere at the BFI IMAX on South Bank, London. The 36-year-old actress, portraying the provocative Sarah Stratton, stunned in a plunging silver g...

item.title: Johannesburg collects nearly R100m after tackling illegal billboards
item.description: The City of Johannesburg has reported a significant increase in revenue, collecting close to R100 million within a few months following a strengthened crackdown on illegal outdoor advertising. The ini...

item.title: OpenAI CEO Altman Discusses AI Safety Concerns with Elon Musk in Testimony
item.description: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, recently provided testimony regarding a discussion he had with Elon Musk about AI safety. The conversation reportedly focused on the potential risks associated with advanced...

item.title: Portraits and Legacy of Chelo Alonso, Icon of 1950s–60s Italian Cinema
item.description: Chelo Alonso, born Isabel Apolonia García Hernández in 1933, was a Cuban-born dancer and actress who rose to fame in the mid-20th century. Known for her magnetic stage presence and extraordinary beaut...

item.title: Odisha Chief Minister reduces convoy size to promote fuel conservation
item.description: In response to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's recent call for judicious use of petrol amid the ongoing oil crisis triggered by tensions in West Asia, Odisha Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi has taken...

item.title: Senator Sotto Criticizes Police for Poor Crowd Management During Senate Rally
item.description: Senator Vicente 'Tito' Sotto expressed his disappointment with the Philippine National Police (PNP) and other security personnel for failing to control the crowd outside the Senate on Wednesday evenin...

item.title: Manchester City Beat Crystal Palace, Keep Pressure on Arsenal in EPL Title Race
item.description: Manchester City don show say dem never ready to gree make Arsenal run away with the English Premier League title. For their rescheduled EPL match against Crystal Palace, City score three solid goals t...

item.title: Passenger restrained after prolonged disruptive behaviour on flight to Cuba
item.description: A flight from Manchester to Cuba was severely disrupted after Zoe Alexander, a mother of one, became aggressive and violent mid-air. According to court reports, Alexander had consumed brandy during th...

item.title: Ontario incurs nearly $200,000 in additional costs after returning private jet to Bombardier
item.description: The Ontario government has released documents detailing expenses related to the purchase and quick resale of a $28.9-million private jet. Premier Doug Ford announced the acquisition of a used 2016 Bom...

item.title: House Republican PAC Supports Progressive Democrat to Challenge Another Democrat in California
item.description: In an unusual political move, the Congressional Leadership Fund, a major political action committee aligned with House Republicans, has endorsed progressive Democrat Randy Villegas in California's 22n...

item.title: List of Young Latina Actresses Gaining Global Recognition in 2026
item.description: The entertainment world continues to welcome a fresh wave of young Latina talent, with several actresses in their 20s rising to international fame as of 2026. Leading the pack are Jenna Ortega and All...

item.title: British couple imprisoned in Iran: family loses contact
item.description: A British couple currently detained in Iran has become the focus of growing concern as their family reports losing all contact with them. Authorities and relatives are increasingly anxious over the co...

item.title: Ghana to Evacuate 300 Citizens from South Africa Following Xenophobic Tensions
item.description: Ghana has announced plans to evacuate 300 of its citizens from South Africa in the wake of rising xenophobic protests targeting foreign nationals. The Ghanaian Foreign Minister, Samuel Okudzeto Ablakw...

item.title: Three Women Die Off Brighton Coast Ahead of The Great Escape Festival; Organisers Confirm Events Continue
item.description: On Wednesday 13th May 2026, The Great Escape festival in Brighton began amid tragic news. Early that morning, emergency services were called to Brighton beach near Madeira Drive, where the bodies of t...

item.title: Larry Walters’ Unconventional Balloon Flight in 1982
item.description: In 1982, 33-year-old California truck driver Larry Walters, famously known as “Lawnchair Larry,” made headlines for an audacious homemade flight. Using an ordinary aluminum lawn chair bought from Sear...

item.title: Chemical leak at Dinmore Meatworks sends five to hospital and prompts mass evacuation
item.description: Early on Thursday morning, emergency services responded to a major chemical leak at the Dinmore Meatworks in Riverview, roughly 30 kilometres south-west of Brisbane. The incident, which involved a sig...

item.title: Tinubu and Kagame agree to strengthen Nigeria-Rwanda trade and cooperation
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don waka go Rwanda to meet President Paul Kagame, wey dem discuss how dem go take deepen relations between Nigeria and Rwanda. Dem gree to revive the Joint Permanent Ministerial ...

item.title: Meghalaya government assures farmers’ safety with additional forces at Assam border
item.description: Meghalaya Deputy Chief Minister Sniawbhalang Dhar on Wednesday assured farmers and activists from Lapangap village, West Jaiñtia Hills, that the state government will deploy additional security person...

item.title: Canada’s Men’s Soccer Team Highlights Multicultural Identity Ahead of 2026 World Cup
item.description: Canada’s participation in the 2026 Men’s World Cup is poised to offer a distinctive representation of national identity, distinct from traditional sporting symbols like hockey. While hockey has histor...
